goal: Frame a small project on recent changes in eating habits.
Include concrete, measurable targets for the pilot (60-person hybrid) to frame the project.
constraints: Incorporate cost per meal considerations and price limits into pilot design.
Base pilot uses free fruit; assess price sensitivity for future iterations.
Internal feasibility constraints: feasibility varies with company size, onsite vs hybrid setup, and budget.
Pilot group size is ~60 opt-in employees; ensure low-burden participation.
Participants are mid-career professionals with busy but somewhat flexible schedules.
Hybrid work prevalence has increased over ~18 months; design must normalize outcomes per onsite-day and include retrospective now-vs-18-month-baseline items.
Receipt data usage requires consent and privacy protections when linking to individuals.
Dietary accommodations: incorporate vegetarian/pescatarian and gluten-free options into pilot design and labeling; ensure cost considerations reflect these options.
Budget matters: ensure any recommended lunch options are inexpensive to accommodate a price-conscious team.
Metrics must be normalized per onsite-days.
Pilot targets are time-bound: baseline 2 weeks; interim 4 weeks; final 8 weeks.
decisions: Include price considerations in the design of the pilot (price per meal, subsidies, price caps, bundles).
Plan minimal fruit-pilot: free fruit bowl + daily reminders; timeline 2 weeks baseline, 8 weeks intervention, 1 week endline; target site of 50–200 employees.

Hypothesis: providing free fruit with reminders increases weekday fruit consumption; primary outcome: per-capita weekday fruit taken per week; measure via inventory depletion counts or POS; predefined success: ≥20% increase in per-capita fruit taken vs baseline or ~0.5 fruit servings per employee per weekday.
Secondary outcomes and data collection: weekly pulse survey; monitor vending purchases; staff satisfaction survey; ensure privacy and consent.
Adapt pilot plan (intervention, metrics, timeline) to company size, onsite vs hybrid setup, and budget.
Recruitment target set at ~60 opt-in participants.
Draft intake + baseline survey including 18-month retrospective items.
Produce a one-page pilot checklist tailored to a 60-person hybrid group (logistics, metrics, timeline).
Integrate workplace vending and delivery receipts data with swipes and diaries.
Consider producing a one-page pilot scorecard template.
entities: Social media, influencers and food culture
Policy, labeling and public health messaging
Circadian and dietary timing awareness (time-restricted eating, intermittent fasting)
dietary preferences tracking and labeling (vegetarian, pescatarian, gluten-free)
daily menu variety to meet dietary mix
open: Assess the impact of seasonality and local availability on short-term behavior change; determine data sources and measurement plan.
Review common pitfalls in workplace pilots aimed at changing eating habits and note mitigations.
Define pricing plan for pilot including price limits and subsidies; plan data reduction/collection for price sensitivity.
Assess internal feasibility: determine company size, onsite vs hybrid, and budget; adjust scope accordingly.
Confirm recruitment target ~60 opt-in participants; align logistics with this size.
Incorporate 18-month retrospective items into intake/baseline survey.
Develop measurement plan to normalize uptake per onsite-day given hybrid schedules.
Plan data pipeline to standardize receipt fields and link to swipes/diaries; define mapping, cleaning, and aggregation rules.
Define privacy, consent, and pseudonymization workflow for linking receipts to participants; ensure aggregated reporting.
Identify KPI updates for receipts: weekly counts, spend, top SKUs, share of items by category; normalization per onsite-days.
Track uptake by dietary category (veg/pesc/GF) via receipts/diaries and adjust procurement accordingly.
Assess supplier capabilities for GF ingredients and cross-contact prevention; update procurement requirements.
Define concrete primary and secondary outcomes with numerical targets and measurement cadence.
Baseline: 2 weeks; Interim: week 4; Final: week 8.
Preregister baseline measurement window and primary outcome formula for evaluation.
Define success thresholds (Green/Yellow/Red) for evaluating pilot outcomes.
Create a deliverable plan for a one-page pilot scorecard template (fields to fill weekly, calculations prepopulated).
Provide guidance on analyzing effect sizes versus statistical significance due to small sample sizes.
